frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Yaar, I always say women should be respected, not because of all this modern equality talk, but because every woman is someone's daughter today and someone's wife and mother tomorrow. If men just remembered that at home, society would automatically become better.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames a woman's worth and entitlement to respect through her roles in relation to men and family, rather than as an individual person. It encodes the surface manifestation of 'respect women because they are someone's daughter/wife/mother' rhetoric.
original_frame: Female Education as Threat, Waste, or Conditional Privilege
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 28. Single. A man in Mumbai. You are on a matrimonial website talking to families for yourself. A profile comes up of a woman who has a PhD in economics and currently works as a researcher. She is 27 and by every measure an exceptional match. You are telling your mother about this profile. Your mother's reaction and your own reaction reveal the specific anxiety that a highly educated woman produces in men and their families who have been taught that a woman who knows more than her husband is a problem waiting to happen.
